title: craibia brevicaudata subsp. baptistarum
intro: Craibia brevicaudata subsp. baptistarum, also known as the African walnut, is a tree species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive walnut-like seeds and aromatic wood. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bark and roots of the tree are used in herbal preparations, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various gastrointestinal issues. The seeds and leaves of the plant are used to make decoctions and infusions, which are consumed for their medicinal properties, including lowering blood pressure and improving digestive health.
